zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Booth於1403年出生在英國藍卡爾阿希頓-德-林時，她的父親Sir Thomas Booth25歲，母親Katherine Booth Assheton32歲。她於1422年與Edmund Pilkington在英國藍卡爾布拉可洛德結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1472年之前在英國藍卡爾皮爾金頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Booth于1403年出生在英国藍卡爾阿希頓-德-林时，她的父亲Sir Thomas Booth25岁，母亲Katherine Booth Assheton32岁。她于1422年与Edmund Pilkington在英国藍卡爾布拉可洛德结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1472年之前在英国藍卡爾皮爾金頓。

nameMeanings.name: Booth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern): topographic or occupational name from Middle English bothe (Old Danish bōth) ‘temporary shelter, such as a covered market stall or a cattle-herdsman's hut’. The latter sense was predominant in the Pennines of Lancashire and Yorkshire, where there were many cattle farms or vaccaries, and whose subdivisions were known as ‘booths’. The principal meaning of the surname there was therefore probably ‘cattle herdsman’, ‘man in charge of a vaccary’, and thus identical with Boothman . Elsewhere it may have denoted a shopkeeper who owned a temporary market stall, but no evidence has been found to confirm this use of the surname. In the British Isles the surname is still more common in northern England, where Scandinavian influence was more marked, and in Scotland, where the word was borrowed into Gaelic as both(an).
History: Robert Booth (1604–72) is mentioned in the colonial records of Exeter, NH, in 1645. He subsequently moved to ME.

variants.name: Boothman
meanings: English (northern): occupational name from Middle English both (Old Danish bōth) ‘temporary shelter, such as a covered market stall or a cattle-herdsman's hut’ + man. In the Pennine uplands of northern England, where booths were subdivisons of vaccaries or cattle-rearing establishment, the name probably denoted a herdsman who looked after cattle, perhaps a man in charge of a vaccary. Elsewhere the surname might have denoted a shopkeeper but evidence for this lacking. Alternatively, the name can also be an occupational name for a servant of a man surnamed Booth .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Booth was born in 1403, in Ashton-under-Lyne, Lancashire, England, her father, Sir Thomas Booth, was 25 and her mother, Katherine Booth Assheton, was 32. She married Edmund Pilkington in 1422, in Blackrod, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. She died before 1472, in Pilkington, Lancashire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Booth est née en 1403, à Ashton-under-Lyne, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Thomas Booth, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Katherine Booth Assheton, avait 32 ans. Elle a épousé Edmund Pilkington en 1422, à Blackrod, Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée avant 1472, à Pilkington, Lancashire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Booth 1403 in Ashton-under-Lyne, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Thomas Booth 25 und ihre Mutter Katherine Booth Assheton 32. Sie hat 1422 in Blackrod, Lancashire, England Edmund Pilkington geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb vor 1472 in Pilkington, Lancashire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Booth è nata il nel 1403 nel Ashton-under-Lyne, Lancashire, England, sua padre, Sir Thomas Booth, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Katherine Booth Assheton, aveva 32 anni. Lei sposato Edmund Pilkington nel 1422, nel Blackrod, Lancashire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Morì prima del 1472, nel Pilkington, Lancashire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Boothが1403年にイギリスランカシュアイアアシュトン-ウンデル-リンで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas Boothは25歳，母Katherine Booth Asshetonは32歳であった。1422年にイギリスランカシュアイアブラクロドでEdmund Pilkingtonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1472年以前にイギリスランカシュアイアピルキントンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Booth(이)가 1403년 영국 랭카실르 애슈토-운더-리니에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Thomas Booth(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Katherine Booth Assheton(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 1422년 영국 랭카실르 블랙로드에서 Edmund Pilkington(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1472년 이전 영국 랭카실르 피킨그턴에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Booth nasceu em 1403, em Ashton-under-Lyne, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Thomas Booth, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Katherine Booth Assheton, tinha 32 anos. Ela casou-se com Edmund Pilkington em 1422, em Blackrod, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu antes 1472, em Pilkington, Lancashire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Booth родилась в 1403 г. в местности аштон-ундир-лин, ланкашир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Thomas Booth было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Katherine Booth Assheton, 32. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Edmund Pilkington в 1422 г. в местности блэккрод, ланкашир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла перед 1472 г. в местности пилкингтон, ланкашир, Англия.
